heading: Carbon removal
label: 16298d Carbon removal
notes: Amendments2021—Subsecs. (j), (k). added subsec. (j) and redesignated former subsec. (j) as (k).Pub. L. 117–58

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary, in coordination with the heads of appropriate Federal agencies, including the Secretary of Agriculture, shall establish a research, development, and demonstration program (referred to in this section as the “program”) to test, validate, or improve technologies and strategies to remove carbon dioxide from the atmosphere on a large scale.

children.heading: Intraagency coordination
children.label: b Intraagency coordination
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the program includes the coordinated participation of the Office of Fossil Energy, the Office of Science, and the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy.
direct air capture and storage technologies;
bioenergy with carbon capture and sequestration;
enhanced geological weathering;
agricultural practices;
forest management and afforestation; and
planned or managed carbon sinks, including natural and artificial.
land use changes, including impacts on natural and managed ecosystems;
ocean acidification;
net greenhouse gas emissions;
commercial viability;
potential for near-term impact;
potential for carbon reductions on a gigaton scale; and
economic cobenefits.

children.heading: Dilute media
children.label: A Dilute media
children.text: The term “dilute media” means media in which the concentration of carbon dioxide is less than 1 percent by volume.

children.heading: Prize competition
children.label: B Prize competition
children.text: The term “prize competition” means the competitive technology prize competition established under paragraph (2).

children.label: I
children.text: is captured directly from the ambient air; and

children.label: II
children.text: is measured at the source of capture and verified at the point of disposal, injection, or utilization.

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: ii Inclusion
children.text: The term “qualified carbon dioxide” includes the initial deposit of captured carbon dioxide used as a tertiary injectant.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: iii Exclusion
children.text: The term “qualified carbon dioxide” does not include carbon dioxide that is recaptured, recycled, and reinjected as part of the enhanced oil and natural gas recovery process.

children.heading: Qualified direct air capture facility
children.label: D Qualified direct air capture facility
I
children.text: uses carbon capture equipment to capture carbon dioxide directly from the ambient air; and

children.label: II
children.text: captures more than 50,000 metric tons of qualified carbon dioxide annually.

children.label: I
children.text: that is deliberately released from naturally occurring subsurface springs; or

children.label: II
children.text: using natural photosynthesis.

children.label: A
children.text: precommercial carbon dioxide capture from dilute media; and

children.label: B
children.text: commercial applications of direct air capture technologies.

children.label: A
children.text: the prize competition process; and

children.label: B
children.text: monitoring and verification procedures for projects selected to receive a prize under the prize competition.

children.label: i
children.text: meet minimum performance standards set by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: meet minimum levels set by the Secretary for the capture of carbon dioxide from dilute media; and

children.label: aa
children.text: be operated on a demonstration scale; and

children.label: bb
children.text: have the potential to achieve significant reduction in the level of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere;

children.label: II
children.text: a successful bench-scale demonstration of a carbon capture technology; or

children.label: III
children.text: an operational carbon capture technology on a commercial scale.

children.heading: Commercial direct air capture projects
children.label: B Commercial direct air capture projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: With respect to projects described in paragraph (2)(B), the Secretary shall award prizes under the prize competition to qualified direct air capture facilities for metric tons of qualified carbon dioxide captured and verified at the point of disposal, injection, or utilization.

children.label: I
children.text: shall be equal for each qualified direct air capture facility selected for a prize under the prize competition; and

children.label: aa
children.text: $180 for qualified carbon dioxide captured and stored in saline storage formations;

children.label: bb
children.text: a lesser amount, as determined by the Secretary, for qualified carbon dioxide captured and stored in conjunction with enhanced oil recovery operations; or

children.label: cc
children.text: a lesser amount, as determined by the Secretary, for qualified carbon dioxide captured and utilized in any activity consistent with .section 45Q(f)(5) of title 26

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: iii Requirement
children.text: The Secretary shall make awards under this subparagraph until appropriated funds are expended.

children.heading: Direct air capture test center
children.label: f Direct air capture test center

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall award grants to 1 or more entities for the operation of 1 or more test centers (referred to in this subsection as a “Center”) to provide distinct testing capabilities for innovative direct air capture and storage technologies.December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: advance research, development, demonstration, and commercial application of direct air capture and storage technologies;

children.label: B
children.text: support large-scale pilot and demonstration projects and test direct air capture and storage technologies; and

children.label: C
children.text: develop front-end engineering design and economic analysis.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall select entities to receive grants under this subsection according to such criteria as the Secretary may develop.

children.heading: Competitive basis
children.label: B Competitive basis
children.text: The Secretary shall select entities to receive grants under this subsection on a competitive basis.

children.label: i
children.text: have access to existing or planned research facilities for direct air capture and storage technologies;

children.label: ii
children.text: are institutions of higher education with established expertise in engineering for direct air capture and storage technologies, or partnerships with such institutions of higher education; or

children.label: iii
children.text: have access to existing research and test facilities for bulk materials design and testing, component design and testing, or professional engineering design.

children.heading: Formula for awarding grants
children.label: 4 Formula for awarding grants
children.text: The Secretary may develop a formula for awarding grants under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each grant awarded under this subsection shall be for a term of not more than 5 years, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: B Renewal
children.text: The Secretary may renew a grant for 1 or more additional 5-year terms, subject to a competitive merit review and the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 6 Termination
children.text: To the extent otherwise authorized by law, the Secretary may eliminate, and terminate grant funding under this subsection for, a Center during any 5-year term described in paragraph (5) if the Secretary determines that the Center is underperforming.
pilot projects that test direct air capture systems capable of capturing 10 to 100 tonnes of carbon oxides per year to provide data for demonstration-scale projects; and
direct air capture demonstration projects capable of capturing greater than 1,000 tonnes of carbon oxides per year.

children.heading: Intraagency collaboration
children.label: h Intraagency collaboration
children.text: In carrying out the program, the Secretary shall encourage and promote collaborations among relevant offices and agencies within the Department.

children.heading: Accounting
children.label: i Accounting
children.text: The Secretary shall collaborate with the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency and the heads of other relevant Federal agencies to develop and improve accounting frameworks and tools to accurately measure carbon removal and sequestration methods and technologies.

children.heading: Regional direct air capture hubs
children.label: j Regional direct air capture hubs

children.heading: Eligible project
children.label: A Eligible project
children.text: The term “eligible project” means a direct air capture project or a component project of a regional direct air capture hub.

children.heading: Regional direct air capture hub
children.label: B Regional direct air capture hub
children.text: The term “regional direct air capture hub” means a network of direct air capture projects, potential carbon dioxide utilization off-takers, connective carbon dioxide transport infrastructure, subsurface resources, and sequestration infrastructure located within a region.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program under which the Secretary shall provide funding for eligible projects that contribute to the development of 4 regional direct air capture hubs described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Regional direct air capture hubs
children.label: B Regional direct air capture hubs
i
children.text: facilitates the deployment of direct air capture projects;

children.label: ii
children.text: has the capacity to capture and sequester, utilize, or sequester and utilize at least 1,000,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere annually from a single unit or multiple interconnected units;

children.label: iii
children.text: demonstrates the capture, processing, delivery, and sequestration or end-use of captured carbon; and

children.label: iv
children.text: could be developed into a regional or interregional carbon network to facilitate sequestration or carbon utilization.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall solicit applications for funding for eligible projects.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Additional solicitations
children.label: ii Additional solicitations
children.text: The Secretary shall solicit applications for funding for eligible projects on a recurring basis after the first round of applications is received under clause (i) until all amounts appropriated to carry out this subsection are expended.

children.heading: Selection of projects for the development of regional direct air capture hubs
children.label: B Selection of projects for the development of regional direct air capture hubs
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the date of the deadline for the submission of proposals under subparagraph (A)(i), the Secretary shall select eligible projects described in paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Carbon intensity of local industry
children.label: i Carbon intensity of local industry
I
children.text: existing carbon-intensive fuel production or industrial capacity; or

children.label: II
children.text: carbon-intensive fuel production or industrial capacity that has retired or closed in the preceding 10 years.

children.heading: Geographic diversity
children.label: ii Geographic diversity
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, eligible projects shall contribute to the development of regional direct air capture hubs located in different regions of the United States.

children.heading: Carbon potential
children.label: iii Carbon potential
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, eligible projects shall contribute to the development of regional direct air capture hubs located in regions with high potential for carbon sequestration or utilization.

children.heading: Hubs in fossil-producing regions
children.label: iv Hubs in fossil-producing regions
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, eligible projects shall contribute to the development of at least 2 regional direct air capture hubs located in economically distressed communities in the regions of the United States with high levels of coal, oil, or natural gas resources.

children.heading: Scalability
children.label: v Scalability
children.text: The Secretary shall give priority to eligible projects that, as compared to other eligible projects, will contribute to the development of regional direct air capture hubs with larger initial capacity, greater potential for expansion, and lower levelized cost per ton of carbon dioxide removed from the atmosphere.

children.heading: Employment
children.label: vi Employment
children.text: The Secretary shall give priority to eligible projects that are likely to create opportunities for skilled training and long-term employment to the greatest number of residents of the region.

children.heading: Additional criteria
children.label: vii Additional criteria
children.text: The Secretary may take into consideration other criteria that, in the judgment of the Secretary, are necessary or appropriate to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: D Coordination
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, in carrying out the program under this subsection, the Secretary shall take into account and coordinate with activities of the carbon capture technology program established under , the carbon storage validation and testing program established under , and the CIFIA program established under such that funding from each of the programs is leveraged to contribute toward the development of integrated regional and interregional carbon capture, removal, transport, sequestration, and utilization networks.section 16292(b)(1) of this titlesection 16293(b)(1) of this titlesection 16372(a) of this title

children.heading: Funding of eligible projects
children.label: E Funding of eligible projects
children.text: The Secretary may make grants to, or enter into cooperative agreements or contracts with, each eligible project selected under subparagraph (B) to accelerate commercialization of, and demonstrate the removal, processing, transport, sequestration, and utilization of, carbon dioxide captured from the atmosphere.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this subsection $3,500,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026, to remain available until expended.

children.label: A
children.text: $15,000,000 shall be used to carry out subsection (e)(2)(A), to remain available until expended; and

children.label: B
children.text: $100,000,000 shall be used to carry out subsection (e)(2)(B), to remain available until expended;
$63,500,000 for fiscal year 2022;
$66,150,000 for fiscal year 2023;
$69,458,000 for fiscal year 2024; and
$72,930,000 for fiscal year 2025.
